title: Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus
taxonomy: Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus is classified in the subfamily Epidendroideae. This subfamily is further divided into several tribes, with Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus being a part of the tribe Cymbidieae. Further classification of Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus can be found at the genus and species level within the tribe Cymbidieae.

intro: Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus, commonly known as the "Mountain Rose" or "Rose of the Andes," is a plant species native to the high-altitude regions of Central and South America, characterized by its fragrant flowers and narrow, radiating leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Dresslerothamnus angustiradiatus include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried flowers and leaves for use in folk remedies and natural cosmetics.
